judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully and correctly answer all three questions; comparable, with Report 1 adding slightly more depth (latent SET vs SET LOCAL note) and Report 2 being crisper on the two-role structure.

report_1.evidence: `cedarline/.env` configures the running FastAPI app's own `DATABASE_URL` to also be `cedar_owner`, not the dedicated `cedar_app` role ... the owner of a table always bypasses row-level security policies unless the table has `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` explicitly set — and `0001_init.sql` never sets `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`

report_2.evidence: `DATABASE_URL` is set to `postgresql://cedar_owner:ownerpw@localhost:5432/cedar` — the schema-owning Postgres role ... a table owner ... is exempt from row-level security policies by default ... since the app connects as the owning role, Postgres silently skips evaluating that policy

report_1.evidence: Change the application's `DATABASE_URL` ... to connect as `cedar_app`, not `cedar_owner`. The app should never hold the owning/migration credentials at runtime. ... Keep migrations running as the owning role (`cedar_owner`) ... owner runs DDL, unprivileged app role runs DML under RLS

report_2.evidence:
`cedar_owner`: used only by `scripts/migrate.sh` / migration tooling, never referenced by the running backend.
- `cedar_app`: the only role the FastAPI app ... ever connects with; it has `SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE` ... but no bypass/owner/superuser privileges, so RLS policies are actually enforced

report_1.evidence: The role `cedar_app` is created solely by `db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql`, which per its own comment ... is a "one-time... run by hand" step, and it is not invoked by `reset-local-db.sh`. So after a wipe+rebuild, `cedar_app` no longer exists ... fails immediately with a "role does not exist" error

report_2.evidence: It never re-runs `cedarline/db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql`. That bootstrap file's own header states it is "Not part of automatic database initialization; run by hand on first setup" ... after any `-v` wipe/rebuild, the `cedar_app` role genuinely no longer exists ... someone must manually re-run `psql ... -f db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql`
